name: tech-stack-evaluator
description: Comprehensive technology stack evaluation and comparison tool with TCO analysis, security assessment, and intelligent recommendations for engineering teams
capabilities: Compare frameworks and languages head-to-head with weighted scoring matrices
Calculate total cost of ownership including hidden costs like technical debt
Assess security posture and compliance readiness for GDPR, SOC2, HIPAA
Analyze migration complexity with effort estimates and risk assessment
Evaluate cloud providers with workload-specific cost and feature analysis
Generate executive summaries with confidence scores and recommendations
author: alirezarezvani
securitySummary: All 219 static findings are false positives. The scanner detected benign documentation patterns including security terminology, code block formatting, and technology names. No actual code execution, network requests, or credential access exists. Uses Python standard library only.
valueStatement: Engineering teams need objective technology decisions but lack tools to compare frameworks, cloud providers, and stacks comprehensively. This skill provides weighted scoring, TCO analysis, security assessment, and migration planning with confidence scores.
userTitle: Evaluate Technology Stacks with Data-Driven Analysis
limitations: Ecosystem metrics are point-in-time snapshots from public databases
TCO calculations use industry defaults when specific data is unavailable
Cannot evaluate closed-source or internal tools without data access
Recommendations assume provided context is accurate and complete

useCases.title: Compare Frameworks for New Project
useCases.description: Evaluate React vs Vue vs Angular for a SaaS dashboard with team skill considerations.

useCases.title: Analyze Cloud Migration Costs
useCases.description: Calculate 5-year TCO for AWS vs Azure including licensing, training, and scaling.

useCases.title: Assess Stack Compliance Readiness
useCases.description: Analyze Express.js plus MongoDB stack for SOC2 and GDPR compliance gaps.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare React vs Vue for building a SaaS dashboard. Focus on developer experience and ecosystem.

promptTemplates.title: Stack Evaluation
promptTemplates.prompt: Evaluate our tech stack for a real-time collaboration platform: Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, WebSockets, AWS. Include TCO analysis and security assessment.

promptTemplates.title: Migration Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Assess migration from Angular.js to React. Our codebase has 50,000 lines, 200 components, and an 8-person team. Need zero-downtime migration.

promptTemplates.title: Cloud Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare AWS vs Azure vs GCP for ML workloads. Priorities: GPU availability 40%, cost 30%, ecosystem 20%, support 10%. Team has 5 ML engineers.

bestPractices: Provide specific use cases and constraints for accurate recommendations
Include team size, skill levels, and timeline constraints
Request specific report sections for focused analysis
Validate TCO assumptions against your actual costs
antiPatterns: Using for trivial decisions between nearly identical tools
Ignoring confidence scores when making final decisions
Comparing more than 5 technologies at once

faq.question: How current is the ecosystem data?
faq.answer: Uses current public data from GitHub, npm, and CVE databases. Metrics are point-in-time snapshots.

faq.question: Can I compare more than 2 technologies?
faq.answer: Yes, supports comparing 2-5 technologies. More than 5 reduces actionability.

faq.question: What if I lack data for TCO analysis?
faq.answer: The skill uses industry-standard defaults. Provide what you know and it fills reasonable estimates.

faq.question: How does confidence scoring work?
faq.answer: Based on score gaps between options, data completeness, and requirement clarity.

faq.question: Can I export reports?
faq.answer: Yes, generates markdown reports that can be saved or exported for documentation.

faq.question: What compliance standards are supported?
faq.answer: GDPR, SOC2, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS with feature checklists and gap analysis.
